title: OECD Framework for Statistics on the Distribution of Household Income, Consumption and Wealth
indicator: income consumption wealth
summary: Joint framework for micro-level income, consumption and wealth statistics.
comparability_notes: Foundational; use with 2024 handbook for updated distributional guidance.

title: OECD Handbook on the Compilation of Household Distributional Results on Income Consumption and Saving in Line with National Accounts Totals
indicator: adjusted disposable income actual consumption saving
summary: Guidance for aligning micro distributions with national accounts totals.
comparability_notes: Includes social transfers in kind; household is primary unit.

title: Distribution of household income, consumption and saving in line with national accounts
indicator: income consumption saving distributions
summary: Methodology and results from the OECD 2020 collection round.
comparability_notes: Country and year coverage must be checked before comparison.

title: Disposable Household Income: Methodology
indicator: disposable household income
summary: Defines LIS disposable household income and exclusions.
comparability_notes: Excludes imputed service flows from housing and durables in key measure.

title: Luxembourg Income Study Database
indicator: labour capital pension transfers taxes expenditures
summary: Harmonised income and demographic microdata across countries.
comparability_notes: Availability and source survey differ by country and wave.

title: Luxembourg Wealth Study Database
indicator: assets liabilities income expenditures
summary: Cross-national harmonised household balance-sheet database.
comparability_notes: Income reference year may differ from wealth reference date.

title: Taxation of Household Savings
indicator: marginal effective tax rates by asset
summary: Compares taxation of deposits, bonds, shares, pensions and housing.
comparability_notes: Tax rules require current-country updates.

title: Income in the United States: 2024
author_or_institution: Kollar and Scherer, U.S. Census Bureau
indicator: money income earnings inequality post-tax estimates
summary: CPS ASEC income and inequality estimates.
comparability_notes: Money income differs from BEA and adjusted disposable-income concepts.

title: Consumer Expenditures—2024
indicator: income expenditures spending categories
summary: Annual income and expenditure results by household characteristics and quintile.
comparability_notes: Consumer unit differs from household; many headline values are means.

title: Distribution of Personal Income
indicator: personal income disposable income consumption saving
summary: Distributes NIPA personal income totals across households.
comparability_notes: 2024 values provisional or experimental; definitions differ from surveys.

title: Economic Well-Being of U.S. Households in 2025
author_or_institution: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
indicator: income margin emergency savings bills credit housing
summary: Measures budget margin, emergency capacity and financial hardship.
comparability_notes: Adult self-report survey is not a complete household balance sheet.

title: 2022 Survey of Consumer Finances
author_or_institution: Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System
indicator: income assets debts net worth pensions
summary: Benchmark U.S. family wealth and financial participation survey.
comparability_notes: Highly skewed distributions require weights, medians and means.

title: The Distribution of Household Income, 2022
indicator: market income social insurance means-tested transfers federal taxes
summary: Decomposes income before and after transfers and federal taxes.
comparability_notes: Data lag and CBO-specific income definitions must be retained.

title: 2025年居民收入和消费支出情况
indicator: disposable income median income consumption categories
summary: National and urban-rural income and consumption aggregates.
comparability_notes: Per-capita national values do not identify typical young households.

title: 居民人均可支配收入的计算方法
indicator: wage business property transfer net income
summary: Defines disposable income components and national household survey design.
comparability_notes: Not identical to OECD adjusted disposable income.

title: 居民人均可支配收入的基础数据来源
indicator: sampling coverage quality targets
summary: Documents target population, sampling and precision goals.
comparability_notes: Aggregated releases limit fine-grained heterogeneity analysis.

title: 中国家庭金融调查（第六轮）研究报告
author_or_institution: 中国家庭金融调查与研究中心
indicator: income expenditure housing assets debt insurance
summary: Comprehensive CHFS report on household financial decisions.
comparability_notes: Full bibliographic and sample details require book-level review.

title: 2021年中国家庭金融调查数据公开发布
author_or_institution: 中国家庭金融调查与研究中心
indicator: employment income expenditure assets debt social security
summary: Public access point for CHFS 2021 microdata.
comparability_notes: Access conditions, weights and cross-wave consistency require review.

title: 2019年中国城镇居民家庭资产负债情况调查
author_or_institution: 中国人民银行调查统计司城镇居民家庭资产负债调查课题组
indicator: housing financial assets liabilities debt service
summary: Survey of more than 30000 urban households by PBOC task group.
comparability_notes: Current URL is a mirror of China Finance article; locate official original.

title: Survey of Income and Housing User Guide: Income
indicator: private gross disposable adjusted and final income
summary: Defines Australian household income concepts and equivalisation.
comparability_notes: Latest complete public SIH reference period is older than current conditions.

title: Household Expenditure Survey Australia: Summary of Results Methodology
indicator: expenditure income wealth housing costs
summary: Defines HES household, income, expenditure and equivalisation methods.
comparability_notes: Detailed survey is infrequent and predates recent price shifts.

title: Australian National Accounts: Household Saving Ratio Methodology
indicator: net saving net disposable income final consumption
summary: Provides macro household saving-ratio definition.
comparability_notes: Sector aggregate cannot show distribution across households.

title: HILDA Statistical Report 2025
indicator: income wealth labour family economic well-being
summary: Longitudinal evidence on economic and family trajectories.
comparability_notes: Selected report topics; full analysis requires microdata and attrition methods.

title: Household Wealth prior to COVID-19: Evidence from the 2018 HILDA Survey
author_or_institution: Adams, Holland, Penrose and Schofer, Reserve Bank of Australia
indicator: assets debts liquid buffers expenses
summary: Links household wealth composition to resilience against income loss.
comparability_notes: Pre-COVID balance sheets and asset prices may not represent current conditions.

title: Retirement Income Review: Final Report
indicator: Age Pension superannuation voluntary saving housing
summary: Fact base on Australia's three-pillar retirement system.
comparability_notes: Retirement focus does not cover all working-age surplus formation.

title: Household Savings and the Superannuation Guarantee
indicator: superannuation and other saving
summary: Studies whether compulsory superannuation substitutes for other household saving.
comparability_notes: Full paper must be reviewed for identification and external validity.

title: Saving and Liquidity Constraints
indicator: assets income shocks consumption
summary: Foundational buffer-stock saving model under borrowing constraints.
comparability_notes: Stylised model assumptions must not be treated as direct country estimates.

title: How Important is Precautionary Saving?
indicator: wealth income uncertainty
summary: Empirical link between income uncertainty and household wealth.
comparability_notes: Requires review of uncertainty measures and sample selection.

title: Household Finance
indicator: participation diversification mortgages financial mistakes
summary: Maps household finance constraints and common decision errors.
comparability_notes: Review article, not a single causal estimate.

title: The Wealthy Hand-to-Mouth
author_or_institution: Greg Kaplan, Giovanni Violante and Justin Weidner
indicator: liquid assets illiquid assets marginal propensity to consume
summary: Identifies households with substantial illiquid wealth but little liquidity.
comparability_notes: Country survey definitions and reference years differ.

title: Towards an Explanation of Household Portfolio Choice Heterogeneity
indicator: income risk stock participation participation costs
summary: Studies fixed participation costs and nonfinancial income in portfolio choice.
comparability_notes: Formal published version and structural assumptions require review.

title: Consumption Inequality and Intra-household Allocations
indicator: earnings consumption sharing
summary: Shows household-level equivalisation can hide internal allocation differences.
comparability_notes: Historical UK evidence cannot be directly transferred to target countries.

title: Why Are Saving Rates of Urban Households in China Rising?
indicator: saving consumption housing education health
summary: Examines rising urban saving and private expenditure burdens.
comparability_notes: Historical urban sample; not a current national estimate.

title: Income Uncertainty and Household Savings in China
author_or_institution: Marcos Chamon, Kai Liu and Eswar Prasad
indicator: income variance pensions saving
summary: Links transitory income uncertainty and pension reform to saving patterns.
comparability_notes: Data and pension regime are historical.

title: The Competitive Saving Motive
indicator: sex ratios household saving marriage market
summary: Tests a marriage-market competitive saving mechanism.
comparability_notes: Strong causal claim requires close review of instruments and alternatives.

title: The Chinese Saving Rate: Long-term Care Risks Family Insurance and Demographics
indicator: saving long-term care family insurance demographics
summary: Quantifies elderly risks and declining family insurance in a structural model.
comparability_notes: Model decomposition is not direct causal identification.

title: Effects of Macroeconomic Shocks: Household Financial Distress Matters
indicator: financial distress credit access shock severity consumption
summary: Studies how pre-existing financial distress shapes macro-shock responses.
comparability_notes: Structural estimates require model and sample review.

title: Self-assessed Life-Cycle Saving Behavior in the U.S. and Singapore
indicator: saving regret procrastination economic shocks mandatory saving
summary: Compares behavioral and shock explanations of under-saving regret.
comparability_notes: Working paper and self-assessed outcome; not yet final peer-reviewed evidence.
